title: Wrong uuid[] array format
author: MihaelIsaev
body:
By @tierracero

> I did this:

```swift
SwifQL.selectAll().from(fiAccountsServices.table).where(fiAccountsServices.$paymentRelation ||> [pst.id])
```

> and it produce

```sql
SELECT * FROM "FIAccountsServices" WHERE "FIAccountsServices"."paymentRelation" @> '34E064BF-F31D-40B0-8036-61B4146EFE54'
```

> but should have produced

```sql
SELECT * FROM "FIAccountsServices" WHERE "FIAccountsServices"."paymentRelation" @> '{34E064BF-F31D-40B0-8036-61B4146EFE54}'
```

> (with brakets {})
> with enums works well

title: Implement Postgres boolean aggregate functions
author: EthanLozano
body: Implement `bool_and` and `bool_or` [aggregate functions](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-aggregate.html)

title: Implement createIndex with column operator class
author: EthanLozano
body:
Extend [IndexItem](https://github.com/SwifQL/SwifQL/blob/2.0.0-beta.3.14.2/Sources/SwifQL/IndexItem.swift#L19) to support  an [operator class](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/indexes-opclass.html).

Postgres allows indexes to specify an operator class. For instance, the [pg_trgm](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/pgtrgm.html) extension could have the following index declaration:
```
CREATE INDEX trgm_idx ON test_trgm USING GIN (t gin_trgm_ops);
```

It appears that IndexItem.column(colum:order:) could have an "opclass" parameter for operator class configuration.

For the time being, I used an updateBuilder and raw sql to create an index with an operator class.

title: Should be SwifQLPartAlias init public?
author: Igor-Palaguta
body: SwifQLPartAlias is declared as public, but it can not be created as init is internal. Can be init publicized?

title: Improve update ... set syntax and describe it in readme
author: MihaelIsaev
body:
Current syntax looks like this
```swift
SwifQL
    .update(SomeTable.table)
    .set[items: Path.Column("col_NEW") == "col_str"]
//    .where(`where`)
    .returning
    .asterisk
```

title: Split update table queries
author: MihaelIsaev
body:
This code is wrong
```swift
// MARK: 2020-10-02
struct AddDeletedAtColumnToAffiliateProductTable: TableMigration {
    typealias Table = AffiliateProduct

    static func prepare(on conn: BridgeConnection) -> EventLoopFuture<Void> {
        updateBuilder
            .addColumn("deleted_at", .timestamptz)
            .createIndex(name: "deleted_at_not_null_idx", items: .column("deleted_at"), where: (\AffiliateProduct.$deletedAt).isNotNull)
            .execute(on: conn)
    }

    static func revert(on conn: BridgeConnection) -> EventLoopFuture<Void> {
        updateBuilder
            .dropIndex(name: "deleted_at_not_null_idx")
            .dropColumn("deleted_at")
            .execute(on: conn)
    }
}
```
it gives error
```bash
ALTER TABLE "conference_groups" ADD COLUMN "deleted_at" timestamptz;CREATE INDEX "deleted_at_null_idx" ON "conference_groups"("deleted_at") WHERE "conference_groups"."deleted_at" IS NULL; [] (PostgresNIO/PostgresDatabase+Query.swift:124)
[ ERROR ] cannot insert multiple commands into a prepared statement (exec_parse_message) (PostgresNIO/Connection/PostgresConnection+Database.swift:56)
```
proper syntax for prepare method:
```swift
static func prepare(on conn: BridgeConnection) -> EventLoopFuture<Void> {
    updateBuilder
        .addColumn("deleted_at", .timestamptz)
        .execute(on: conn).flatMap {
            updateBuilder
                .createIndex(name: "deleted_at_null_idx", items: .column("deleted_at"), where: (\AffiliateProduct.$deletedAt).isNull)
                    .execute(on: conn)
        }
}
```
**seems that we could split query executions to different calls instead of one separated by ; to avoid this situation**

title: Help needed to fetch record as nested json
author: jazeelqb
body:
I have some model classes as follows:
```swift
final class User: Tableable {
    var id: UUID?
    var name: String
    var email: String
    var mobile: String
    var username: String
    var password: String
    var userRole: UserRole
}
```

```swift
final class Center: Tableable {
    var id: Int?
    var name: String
}
```

```swift
final class Course: Tableable {
    var id: Int?
    var name: String
}
```

```swift
final class Batch: Tableable {
    var id: Int?
    var name: String
    var courseId: Course.ID
    var centerId: Center.ID
}
```

```swift
final class SupervisorBatch: Tableable {
    var id: Int?
    var supervisorId: User.ID
    var batchId: Batch.ID
}
```

What I'm trying to figure out is a way to query the User table to get records in the below format:

```json
{
  "mobile": "0123456789",
  "id": "65EF4CD4-E596-4FAA-AF15-F297894F38AC",
  "userRole": "supervisor",
  "userStatus": "active",
  "email": "bc1@xxxx.com",
  "batches": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "batch 1",
      "course": {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "course 1"
      },
      "center": {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "center 1",
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "name": "batch 2",
      "course": {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "course 1"
      },
      "center": {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "center 1",
      }
    }
  ],
  "name": "BC1"
}
```

So far I could only get to this code:

```swift
let query = SwifQLSelectBuilder()
query.select(User.table.*)
query.select(Fn.array_agg(Fn.to_jsonb(Batch.table)) => "batches")
query.where(\User.userRole == UserRole.supervisor)
query.groupBy(\User.id, \SupervisorBatch.supervisorId)
query.from(User.table)
query.join(.left, SupervisorBatch.table, on: \User.id == \SupervisorBatch.supervisorId)
query.join(.left, Batch.table, on: \SupervisorBatch.batchId == \Batch.id)
query.join(.left, Course.table, on: \Course.id == \Batch.courseId)
query.join(.left, Center.table, on: \Center.id == \Batch.centerId)
query.orderBy(.asc(\User.name))

return query.build().execute(on: request, as: .psql).all(decoding: SupervisorResponse.self)

struct SupervisorResponse: Content {
    let id: User.ID?
    let name: String
    let email: String
    let mobile: String
    let userRole: UserRole
    let userStatus: UserStatus
    let batches: [Batch]
}

final class Batch: Tableable {
    var id: Int?
    var name: String
    var courseId: Course.ID
    var centerId: Center.ID
}
```

I am not able to join the Course and Center tables to Batch using the above code. Any help on this is greatly appreciated. I have been stuck on this for a couple of days now, being a newbie to psql.

title: Reflection Problem
author: hiimtmac
body:
Given this model:
```swift
public struct T: Codable, Reflectable, Tableable {
    let name: String
    let rate: Double

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case name
        case rate
    }

    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
        self.name = try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name)
        self.rate = try container.decodeIfPresent(Double.self, forKey: .rate) ?? 0.0
    }
}
```

If one of the properties in the `init(decoder:)` method is optionally decoded and provided a default value like `rate` above, when you try and access it in your query:
```swift
let t = T.as("t")
t~\.rate // is NOT "rate"

or

\T.rate // is NOT "rate"
```
Normal optionals work fine (`try container.decodeIfPresent(Double.self, forKey: .rate)`) but as soon as you add the default value (`?? 0.0`) it does not work.

Any idea why this might be or am I doing something wrong?

[Failing Test](https://github.com/hiimtmac/SwifQLBug/blob/fe8749c43482b8daf32a8834e5f6249ce7dd18b0/Tests/SwifQLBugTests/SwifQLBugTests.swift#L24) | [Example repo](https://github.com/hiimtmac/SwifQLBug)

title: public init for OrderByItem
author: Igor-Palaguta

title: How to use PostgreSQL earth_box query with this lib
author: aonawale
body:
I need to use the earth distance module to perform a location-based query. Does this lib support it? If true, how do I use it? My query looks like this:

``` swift
let builder = SwifQLSelectBuilder()

builder.select(Request.table.*)

builder.from(Request.table)

let rsp = RequestServicePivot.as("request_service")
let usp = UserServicePivot.as("service_user")
let rap = RequestAddressPivot.as("address_request")

builder.join(.inner, rsp.table, on: rsp~\.requestID == \Request.id)
builder.join(.inner, usp.table, on: usp~\.serviceID == rsp~\.serviceID)
builder.join(.inner, rap.table, on: rap~\.requestID == \Request.id)
builder.join(.inner, Address.table, on: \Address.id == rap~\.addressID)

builder.where(
    \Request.userID != userID &&
    |\Request.artisanID == userID || \Request.status => .text == status|
    && earth_box(ll_to_earth($3, $4), $5) @> ll_to_earth(address."latitude", address."longitude")
)

builder.orderBy(.desc(\Request.status), .desc(\Request.createdAt))
builder.limit(limit)
builder.offset(offset)
```

This is the location clause `earth_box(ll_to_earth($3, $4), $5) @> ll_to_earth(address."latitude", address."longitude")`

title: Cannot join and decode Date field
author: davuthdv
body:
I have models that looks something like this
```
struct Item: Model {
    var id: UUID?
    var name: String
    var imageId: UUID?
}

struct Image: Model {
    var id: UUID?
    var url: String
    var createdAt: Date?
}
```

I create a query that does a `LEFT JOIN` from `Item` to `Image` and try to decode result into this struct:
```
struct ItemFull: Decodable {
    var id: UUID?
    var name: String
    var image: Image?
}
```
... and I got back an error `The data couldn’t be read because it isn’t in the correct format.`

So I create a new struct similar to `Image` but without the `Date` field:
```
struct ImageWithoutDate: Decodable {
    var id: UUID?
    var url: String
}
```
... and replace `var image: Image?` to `var image: ImageWithoutDate?`, it works fine. It seems that there's an issue while trying to decode the date field. Or am I missing something like date formatter somewhere?

title: No comma should be after DISTINCT ON
author: Igor-Palaguta
body:
`SwifQL.select([Distinct(on: \User.id), \User.firstName]).from(User.table)`
generates
`SELECT DISTINCT ON ("users"."id"), "users"."firstName" from "users"`
but should be without comma after distinct on:
`SELECT DISTINCT ON ("users"."id") "users"."firstName" from "users"`

title: having generates unneeded and condition
author: Igor-Palaguta
body: Looks like `.having(some_condition)` generates `HAVING some_condition AND some_condition` `
